content:
{
    "concerns": [
        {
            "id": "SKP-001",
            "concern": "Probe-gate creates a hard dependency and new failure surface on every multi-model call",
            "severity": "CRITICAL",
            "severity_score": 850,
            "why_matters": "AC-1.2 requires pinging every provider before any multi-model run. If providers throttle the probe traffic, treat probes as abuse, or have probe endpoints with different reliability than actual inference endpoints, the gate becomes a self-inflicted outage. The 60s cache helps but doesn't eliminate the problem on cold paths or cron jobs. Probes also don't validate the actual inference path (different endpoints, different rate limit pools).",
            "location": "Section 4.1 AC-1.2; Section 5.1 NFR-Perf-1",
            "recommendation": "Define probe semantics precisely: which endpoint, which auth, which rate-limit bucket. Add fail-open mode for probe failures (probe is advisory, not gating) and document when probe-gate itself is the source of degradation. Add metrics to detect probe-induced false negatives."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-002",
            "concern": "Capability-class taxonomy will not survive contact with reality",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 720,
            "why_matters": "The 9 declared classes (top-reasoning-openai, top-anthropic-frontier, etc.) hardcode a vendor-organized worldview that vendors themselves keep changing. When OpenAI ships a non-reasoning frontier model, or Anthropic blurs reasoning/non-reasoning, the taxonomy fragments. Gates declaring `class: top-reasoning-anthropic` will need migration with every vendor reorg — exactly the brittleness this cycle claims to fix.",
            "location": "Section 4.2 AC-2.1",
            "recommendation": "Define classes by capability properties (context window, reasoning depth, latency tier) rather than vendor lineage, OR explicitly accept that the taxonomy will require quarterly review and add a class-migration AC."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-003",
            "concern": "Strict failure-as-non-zero (L1) will cause production CI/CD pain",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 700,
            "why_matters": "Any multi-model degradation = non-zero exit means a single provider blip blocks PR merges, audit-sprint runs, and review-sprint gates. In practice operators will set `LOA_FORCE_LEGACY_MODELS=1` permanently to avoid the noise (R6 already flags this risk). The cycle's own escape hatch undermines its core thesis.",
            "location": "Section 0 L1; AC-1.5; Risk R6",
            "recommendation": "Distinguish 'degraded but completed with fallback' (warn, exit 0) from 'unable to complete' (exit non-zero). Strict failure should apply to chain exhaustion (BUDGET_EXHAUSTED) not to any degradation. Reconsider L1's blanket policy."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-004",
            "concern": "Open-ended timeline gated on M1-M8 has no abort criteria",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 680,
            "why_matters": "Section 2.2 states 'open-ended... gate on M1-M8, not wall-clock.' M1 requires '0 silent degradations in 30-day window' which means the cycle cannot ship until 30 days of clean operation are observed. This creates an indefinite cycle with no failure mode — if M1 trips at day 28, do you restart the window? Five sprints with no time budget historically slip.",
            "location": "Section 2.2",
            "recommendation": "Define maximum cycle duration, define what happens if M1 keeps tripping (does cycle pause? extend? declare partial success?), and add explicit ship/no-ship decision points after each sprint."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-005",
            "concern": "Drift-CI regex for hardcoded model names will produce constant false positives",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 640,
            "why_matters": "AC-3.3 regex `claude-[0-9]|gpt-[0-9]|gemini-[0-9]|opus|sonnet|haiku` will match documentation, comments, test fixtures, error messages, audit log examples, NOTES.md archaeology entries, and the migration guide itself. The allowlist approach (per cycle-099 raw-curl precedent) becomes a maintenance treadmill. Heredoc-state handling (NFR-Drift-1) is non-trivial in bash.",
            "location": "AC-3.3; NFR-Drift-1",
            "recommendation": "Use AST-aware scanning where possible, scope the gate to specific path globs (config files, gate definitions), and explicitly exclude markdown/test fixtures by default. Audit cycle-099's raw-curl allowlist size as a leading indicator."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-006",
            "concern": "Deleting model-adapter.sh.legacy in Sprint 4 is irreversible and premature",
            "severity": "HIGH",
            "severity_score": 650,
            "why_matters": "L12/AC-4.2 deletes the 1018 LOC legacy substrate during Sprint 4, but A5 admits the kill switch (LOA_FORCE_LEGACY_MODELS=1) is supposed to restore legacy behavior 'via resolver shim, not by un-deleting'. If the shim is incomplete and a downstream operator hits a regression, recovery requires git archaeology. The 'soft migration' (L7) is undermined by hard deletion of the rollback substrate.",
            "location": "L12; AC-4.2; A5",
            "recommendation": "Quarantine the legacy adapter in an `archive/` path for at least one cycle post-ship. Verify kill-switch shim covers 100% of legacy code paths via test corpus before deletion. Move deletion to a follow-on cycle."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-007",
            "concern": "BB TypeScript codegen-from-SoT introduces build-pipeline coupling that may not work",
            "severity": "MEDIUM",
            "severity_score": 580,
            "why_matters": "AC-4.3 requires yq → ts → bundle codegen. A3 admits 'If wrong: BB stays on manually-edited truncation.ts; AC-4.3 deferred.' This is a known-unknown that could collapse the SoT thesis (M2: 'adapter divergence count = 0'). If BB can't consume codegen, divergence persists and M2 cannot be met.",
            "location": "AC-4.3; A3; M2",
            "recommendation": "Spike the codegen pipeline in Sprint 1 (not Sprint 4) to validate feasibility before committing to the SoT contract. Define what M2 means if BB stays on manual truncation.ts."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-008",
            "concern": "Smoke-fleet weekly cadence is too slow to meet M5 (24h detection latency)",
            "severity": "MEDIUM",
            "severity_score": 560,
            "why_matters": "AC-5.3 specifies weekly cron, but M5 demands <24h between vendor breakage and operator surfacing. AC-5.4 adds a 2-consecutive-failure threshold (R4), which on weekly cadence means up to 2 weeks to surface a regression. The math doesn't work.",
            "location": "M5; AC-5.3; AC-5.4; R4",
            "recommendation": "Either increase smoke-fleet cadence to daily (re-evaluate A4 rate-limit assumption) or relax M5 to match weekly cadence (~14 days). The two cannot coexist."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-009",
            "concern": "model_aliases_extra extension lacks security boundary for operator-supplied config",
            "severity": "MEDIUM",
            "severity_score": 520,
            "why_matters": "AC-2.3 + NFR-Sec-4 mention endpoint-validator on URL fields, but `model_aliases_extra` could include arbitrary `api_id`, `endpoint_family`, capability flags, fallback chains. A malicious or misconfigured extension could redirect a top-reasoning-anthropic class to a compromised endpoint, exfiltrating prompts. No signing, no allowlist of trusted endpoint domains is mentioned.",
            "location": "AC-2.3; NFR-Sec-4; L8",
            "recommendation": "Define a strict schema for `model_aliases_extra`: allowlisted endpoint domains, required provider field matching known providers, prohibition on overriding existing class members (only register new classes/models). Add audit event for alias registration."
        },
        {
            "id": "SKP-010",
            "concern": "Subsumption of cycle-099 (L5) creates ledger and artifact reconciliation risk",
            "severity": "MEDIUM",
            "severity_score": 480,
            "why_matters": "Cycle-099's open Sprint 4 deliverables and `#710` endgame are 'absorbed' into cycle-102. A1 admits this could leave 'phantom open issues left dangling.' If cycle-099 had unmerged PRs, in-flight reviews, or contracts referenced by other cycles (cycle-100 runs in parallel per L3), cross-cycle dependencies become tangled. The 'closes cycle-099' decision is administrative but the technical artifacts span both.",
            "location": "Section 0; L5; A1; cycle-100 parallel per L3",
            "recommendation": "Document explicit migration of every cycle-099 open artifact (issues, PRs, sprint deliverables) at cycle-102 kickoff. Define cycle-100/cycle-102 interface contract since they run in parallel and both touch model integration."
        }
    ],
    "summary": "10 concerns identified, 1 CRITICAL, 5 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM. Top risks: probe-gate as new failure surface, capability-class taxonomy fragility, strict-failure CI noise undermining its own kill switch, and open-ended timeline with no abort criteria."
}
